organizations: Anya internal setup only
success_criterion: All 8 pre-launch tasks checked (Gist verified; Bitly configured; Gmail labels created; response templates saved; tracking spreadsheet pre-populated; contact list built; deliverability tested; send-from address confirmed)
notes: Hard gate — do not send any emails until all 8 tasks are complete

organizations: NILC; CLINIC; RAICES; ILRC; National Lawyers Guild
success_criterion: All 5 Day-1 named organizations contacted; personalized opening in each email; Bitly click on at least 1 by Day 7
notes: Highest-confidence contact info; send first to establish baseline deliverability signal

organizations: 5 regional immigration legal aid organizations (research using [city] immigration legal aid)
success_criterion: At least 3 of 5 confirmed delivered (no bounce); 1+ click by Day 7
notes: Research contacts the Sunday before Week 1; pre-populate spreadsheet. Rotate to email variant B (different subject line)

organizations: 5 more regional immigration legal aid organizations
success_criterion: At least 3 of 5 confirmed delivered; track open signals vs. clicks
notes: Use email variant C (third subject line); compare click rates to Days 1 and 2 variants

organizations: 5 law school immigration clinics (university-based)
success_criterion: At least 2 of 5 confirmed delivered; at least 1 engagement or acknowledgment response by Day 14
notes: Lead with primary-source citeability and client intake supplement use case for law school framing

organizations: 5 public defender offices with immigration units
success_criterion: At least 3 of 5 confirmed delivered; Day 7 check for bounce pattern before Week 2
notes: Research: [city] public defender immigration; confirm office-level contact rather than general office number

organizations: All Week 1 sends
success_criterion: Classify all 25 contacts by score 0-5; resolve bounces; send subject-line follow-up to Score 2 contacts who opened but did not click
notes: This is the most important mid-campaign review. Low response at Day 7 triggers a framing adjustment before Week 2 sends.

organizations: CASA (casaforall.org); Make the Road America; United We Dream; Centro de los Derechos del Migrante; 1 local interfaith sanctuary network
success_criterion: At least 3 of 5 confirmed delivered; at least 1 engagement or acknowledgment by Day 14
notes: Named national organizations — higher response rate expected. Switch to Template 1B framing (accessibility-forward; no tech jargon in opening).

organizations: 5 local interfaith sanctuary networks (research using [city] sanctuary network)
success_criterion: At least 3 of 5 confirmed delivered; track response type
notes: Sanctuary networks often have Signal-based channels; note in tracking spreadsheet if Signal is the better channel for follow-up

organizations: 5 community legal education programs (embedded in churches; community centers; immigrant service agencies)
success_criterion: At least 2 of 5 confirmed delivered
notes: Harder to find direct contact for these; web forms are common — note as web form sends in tracking spreadsheet

organizations: National Bail Fund Network; 4 local/regional mutual aid networks
success_criterion: At least 3 of 5 confirmed delivered; at least 1 click or forward by Day 14
notes: Switch to Template 1C (shorter; Signal-native format). These audiences have security culture — no need to explain what Signal is.

organizations: 5 mutual aid networks (Food Not Bombs chapters; Anarchist Black Cross affiliates; local mutual aid)
success_criterion: At least 3 of 5 confirmed delivered
notes: Template 1C. Post directly in Signal/Slack/Discord channels if you have access — outperforms cold email for these networks.

organizations: All Week 2 sends
success_criterion: Classify all Week 2 contacts by score 0-5; Week 1 follow-ups due for non-responders (Template R2)
notes: If response rate across both weeks is below 5% (fewer than 3 responses from 50 sends), trigger Scenario A pivot review before continuing to Week 3

organizations: 3-5 remaining new contacts (any subtier)
success_criterion: At least 2 confirmed delivered; begin Week 1 follow-up batch
notes: Lower pace — Week 3 is primarily catch-up and follow-up management

organizations: Week 1 sends that have not responded (Score 1 contacts)
success_criterion: Send Template R2 to all non-responders from Days 1-3; update tracking spreadsheet
notes: Maximum 2 contacts per organization (1 initial + 1 follow-up). Never send a third email.

organizations: 3-5 remaining new contacts + follow-up batch
success_criterion: Remaining new sends complete; follow-up sent to Week 1 Days 4-5 non-responders
notes: By end of Day 17 the full contact list of 50+ should be exhausted for initial sends

organizations: All remaining follow-ups
success_criterion: All tracking spreadsheet rows have a final status (Sent; Response type; or Follow-up sent); Week 3 log complete
notes: Close out any remaining sends; review spreadsheet completeness before beginning Tier 2 preparation

organizations: Week 2 sends non-responders; Week 3 non-responders
success_criterion: Template R2 sent to all Week 2 and Week 3 non-responders; all engagement responses answered (Template R1) within 24 hours
notes: ~2-3 hours this week; no new outreach

organizations: Week 3 and 4 non-responders; final response handling
success_criterion: Final response handling complete; all contacts classified with final status; Gate 2 assessment documented
notes: At end of Week 5, assess Gate 2 criteria: volume (40+ contacted); response signal (10%+ rate or 3+ Score-3 contacts); no critical failure

organizations: Internal preparation only — no outreach
success_criterion: Top 5 Tier 2A contacts researched and verified; Tier 2 email templates personalized; May 2026 threat briefings attached per TIER2_MESSAGING_TEMPLATES.md; sector-specific briefing files prepared
notes: This preparation runs parallel to Tier 1 follow-up; ~2 hours/week

target_launch_offset_label: Week 5 (or Week 4 if T1 high-response scenario)
organizations: EFF; CDT; Access Now; Privacy International; Tor Project
success_criterion: At least 3 of 5 confirmed delivered; at least 1 acknowledgment from a named digital rights organization by Day 42
notes: Highest amplification reach. Lead with subject line variants from TIER2_MESSAGING_TEMPLATES.md. Attach tier2-digital-rights-threat-briefing.md.

organizations: Mozilla Foundation; STOP; EPIC; Center for Digital Democracy; Restore the Fourth; Fight for the Future; Demand Progress
success_criterion: At least 4 of 7 confirmed delivered; track response type (Acknowledged vs. Engagement vs. Decline)
notes: Second 2A wave. Use engagement data from first 5 to refine subject line. EPIC particularly relevant due to FOIA-source alignment with corpus methodology.

organizations: All 2A sends
success_criterion: Classify all 2A contacts by score; at least 2 Score 3+ contacts (clicks or replies) to confirm Tier 2 framing is working before beginning 2D
notes: If no Score 3+ contacts after 12 sends: adjust subject line; check whether attach-or-link decision for threat briefings needs revision

organizations: Freedom of the Press Foundation; IRE; CPJ; Reporters Committee for Freedom of the Press; SPJ; NAHJ; AAJA
success_criterion: At least 4 of 7 confirmed delivered; FPF response is highest-value outcome (SecureDrop integration potential); at least 1 acknowledgment from any journalist org by Day 56
notes: Attach tier2-journalists-threat-briefing.md. Lead with source protection angle; secondary angle is threat model as background for reporting. News cycles move fast — send 2D early.

organizations: CMU CyLab; UC Berkeley CLTC; MIT CSAIL/IPRI; UW Security Lab; Stanford Cyber Policy Center; Harvard Berkman Klein; Georgia Tech IISP; RPI CCC; Northeastern CPI
success_criterion: At least 6 of 9 confirmed delivered; at least 1 curriculum interest or research engagement signal by Day 70
notes: Slower response cycles — semester-driven. Attach tier2-academic-cybersecurity-threat-briefing.md. Lead with peer-review invitation framing; secondary is curriculum value. Do NOT send during May-August; delay to September if timing conflicts.

organizations: DEF CON community; CCC; Black Hat CFP; ShmooCon; 3-5 individual surveillance researchers
success_criterion: At least 1 researcher provides substantive technical feedback (validation or corrections) by Day 84; at least 1 conference channel submission acknowledged
notes: Attach tier2-researcher-communities-threat-briefing.md. Peer-to-peer framing required — treat as reviewer not audience. CTA is technical review, not distribution.

organizations: 2A non-responders; any 2D non-responders
success_criterion: Template R2 sent to all non-responders from 2A and 2D first sends; engagement responses answered within 24 hours
notes: Maximum 2 contacts per organization

organizations: 2B non-responders
success_criterion: Template R2 sent to 2B non-responders; Gate 3 criteria assessed at end of Week 10
notes: At end of Week 10: assess Gate 3 criteria: volume (25+ Tier 2 contacts); institutional validation signal (1+ acknowledged from EFF/CDT/Access Now/FPF/academic); 6-week minimum from T2 launch

organizations: Internal preparation only
success_criterion: Priority 3A contacts researched and verified (Georgetown CPT; ACLU SPT; Brennan Center; New America OTI); Tier 3 emails personalized with specific org publication references; any Tier 2 citations documented for Tier 3 credibility compounding
notes: This preparation runs parallel to Tier 2 follow-up; ~2 hours/week. Harder research than T2 — staff turnover in policy organizations is high.

organizations: Georgetown Center on Privacy & Technology; ACLU Speech Privacy & Technology Project; Brennan Center for Justice; New America/OTI; Brookings CTI
success_criterion: At least 3 of 5 confirmed delivered; Georgetown CPT and ACLU SPT are priority — engagement from either constitutes strong Gate 3 success
notes: Georgetown CPT: reference American Dragnet directly — corpus is companion work. ACLU SPT: frame around their litigation against warrantless commercial data purchases. Attach any T2 citation if available.

organizations: CNAS; Aspen Digital; EPIC; Center for Constitutional Rights; Cato Institute Civil Liberties Project; R Street Institute; Future of Privacy Forum
success_criterion: At least 4 of 7 confirmed delivered; at least 1 policy organization begins internal review (any score 3+ contact)
notes: CNAS: frame around government data integrity and rule-of-law (not civil liberties). CATO/R Street: Fourth Amendment angle for cross-aisle reach.

organizations: AFL-CIO Technology Institute; CWA/CODE-CWA; SEIU; SAG-AFTRA; National Domestic Workers Alliance; United Farm Workers; Alphabet Workers Union
success_criterion: At least 4 of 7 confirmed delivered; NDWA and UFW are highest priority (membership directly in scope of ELITE targeting); at least 1 labor org requests adapted member education version
notes: Lead with Part 0 direct member protection angle for NDWA and UFW. AFL-CIO Tech Institute: connect to AI Principles for Workers and commercial data privacy angle.

target_launch_offset_label: Weeks 14-18 (semester timing: target September-October or February-March)
organizations: Harvard Cyberlaw Clinic; Yale ISP/Privacy Lab; Stanford CodeX; NYU Tech Law & Policy Clinic; UW Law Tech Policy Lab; Columbia/Knight Institute; Michigan Law Civil Rights Litigation Initiative; Berkeley Law BCLT; American University IGL; Georgetown Law CPT
success_criterion: At least 6 of 10 confirmed delivered; at least 1 law clinic expresses interest in corpus as case study or research resource by Day 140
notes: Semester timing is critical — do NOT send during May-August. If Week 14 lands in May-August, delay 3C to September. Reference Georgetown Law CPT separately from Georgetown Center on Privacy & Technology (related but distinct programs).

organizations: 3A and 3B non-responders
success_criterion: Template R2 sent to all non-responders from 3A and 3B first sends (use monthly cadence for Tier 3 — not weekly)
notes: Tier 3 follow-up cadence is monthly (not 10-day). These organizations operate on long timelines; a 10-day follow-up will feel pushy.

organizations: 3C non-responders; second-round outreach with any accumulated T2 endorsements
success_criterion: At least 1 academic law/policy program engagement logged; Gate 4 (quarterly review) check-in scheduled
notes: Second-round outreach: for organizations that did not respond initially, a follow-up email that references a Tier 2 or 3A publication/citation has materially higher conversion probability

target_launch_offset_label: July 26 2026 (fixed date regardless of campaign timing)
organizations: All previously contacted organizations if material updates needed
success_criterion: Corpus reviewed for accuracy (FISA 702 status; Palantir contracts; DOGE litigation; GrapheneOS supported devices); update Gist if material changes identified; brief update email sent to Score 3+ contacts from T1 and T2 if updated
notes: This is a fixed date tied to corpus currency, not campaign position. Run this review regardless of where you are in the outreach sequence.
